# 

# Introduction {#introduction}

Welcome\!

We’re thrilled to have you join us at the UC Berkeley AI Hackathon\! All of us are super excited to kick off the fourth iteration of this exciting event, and you all are the driving force behind our event. We want to do everything we can to help you navigate the event; with that said, **please be sure to read through the entire guide\!**

This year, UC Berkeley AI Hackathon is taking place in **UC Berkeley** at the **ASUC Student Union: Martin Luther King Jr. Building**\! In this guide, you’ll find resources on navigating the surrounding area, and itinerary of the workshops, and events we’ll be hosting\!

Whether you're an experienced hacker or just beginning your AI journey, this hackathon is the perfect opportunity to develop your skills and build meaningful connections. The UC Berkeley AI Hackathon wouldn’t be possible without the support from our community, from our incredible sponsors, judges, mentors, and volunteers; with their support, we’re excited to provide you with the resources you need to build out your projects from ideation to reality. We encourage you to check out our workshops, brought to you by our sponsors and Berkeley student organizations, and to reach out to our mentors — there’s no better way to discover new ways to build\!

What truly makes our events special, though, is **you**. Every hacker brings a wealth of unique experiences and ideas; it’s your curiosity, creativity, and love for building that drives us to put on this event. Whether you come to Berkeley with a team or discover one when you arrive, the best ideas are formed from creative minds coming together to build something incredible. The projects that come out of our hackathons never fail to amaze; you inspire us every day to make our events the best it can possibly be. Thank you so much for coming — we can’t wait to see what you build.

Happy hacking\!

Sincerely,  
The Hackathons @ Berkeley Team 🚀🧸

# General Info & Venue {#general-info-&-venue}

## 

## Important Links {#important-links}

Below are some links you may find helpful throughout the event:

- Schedule & Live Site: [live.hackberkeley.org](https://live.hackberkeley.org/)  
- Slack: [hackberkeley.org/slack](https://join.slack.com/t/ai-hackathon-2026-hq/shared_invite/zt-407agvgcy-D0ynSBLzNUboORwueqOplw)  
- Devpost: [https://ai-hackathon-2026.devpost.com](https://ai-hackathon-2026.devpost.com/)  
- Hackathons @ Berkeley App:  
  * App Store: [click here](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/hackathons-berkeley/id6761351929)  
    * Play Store: [click here](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=io.calhacks.hive)

## Directions & Maps {#directions-&-maps}

The UC Berkeley AI Hackathon will take place at:

ASUC Student Union: Martin Luther King Jr. Building  
2495 Bancroft Way, Berkeley, CA 94720

Participants will only be permitted to enter via the second floor entrance from Sproul Plaza, which is also an accessible entrance. **Check-in will begin promptly at 9 AM** on Saturday, June 20\. **Opening Ceremony will begin at 10 AM in Wheeler Auditorium.**

**First-time hackers will be able to check-in early at 8:30 AM** and will have a dedicated intro workshop from 9-10 AM\! More info will be sent to those who qualify directly.

Venue Map: View it on our [Live Site](https://live.hackberkeley.org/#map)\!

## Transportation {#transportation}

BART: The ASUC Student Union: Martin Luther King Jr. Building is conveniently located a 15 minute walk from the Downtown Berkeley BART station on Shattuck, and provides transportation across the Bay Area. Check out the [Downtown Berkeley BART station website](https://www.bart.gov/stations/dbrk) for more information on the station and BART lines it serves. If you plan on taking the BART, we highly recommend planning out your route in advance or traveling with another hacker.

![][image1]

The nearest BART Station is the Downtown Berkeley BART Station. From there, several bus lines can take you to Telegraph and Durant, which is one block from the MLK Student Union.

CAR: There are a number of public garages and limited street parking nearby. We cannot provide parking passes and cannot guarantee overnight parking is available in those places.

## Food Schedule {#food-schedule}

Here is a general overview of the food that will be served at the event.  
See [bit.ly/ai26food](http://bit.ly/ai26food) for more details.  
(We will have vegetarian options\! Please check [bit.ly/ai26food](http://bit.ly/ai26food) or find a director if any allergy concerns arise.)

 

- Saturday Lunch: Ike’s Sandwiches 🥪   
- Saturday Dinner: Himalayan Bite 🥘  
- Saturday Midnight Snack: Insomnia Cookies & Energy Drinks 🍪  
- Sunday Breakfast: Panera Bread Bagels and Pastries 🥐   
- Sunday Lunch: Tacos 🌮  

## 

## Hacker Checklist {#hacker-checklist}

Our venue will be open throughout the hackathon with overnight in-out access, so most hackers choose to bring a sleeping bag and rest at the hacking space overnight\! It’s a hallmark of the hackathon experience :). Though we’re excited to have you, it is NOT MANDATORY to stay overnight. Feel free to work out your own sleeping arrangements (staying with a friend, purchasing a hotel room, etc.) OR *join us overnight in the venue\!*

Please note we aren’t able to offer any lodging outside the event hours and are NOT reimbursing any hotel/travel/overnight expenses.

# 

# 

# 

# 

## Prohibited Items {#prohibited-items}

Please note that the following items are NOT allowed at the venue and are subject to confiscation, removal from the building, escalation to UCPD, and permanent blacklist from our events.

* No flammable items, including but not limited to gasoline, gas torches, lighter fluid, etc.  
* No weapons (guns, knives, pocket knives, fireworks, etc.)  
* No illegal drugs or alcoholic beverages  
* No tents  
* Anything on the list of [UCPD prohibited items here](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1t7id7NAndZcr9JPqHljZjhpHxA0N1mMi/edit?usp=sharing&ouid=103520015645570244494&rtpof=true&sd=true).

# 

# Hacking {#hacking}

## Registration & Team Formation {#registration-&-team-formation}

- Every hacker must be registered to participate\!  
- Teams can be anywhere between 1-4 people; no more than 4 people allowed, and teams can be made up of both undergraduates and new grads if applicable.  
- No team? No problem\! Hop into the \#1-team-formation channel on Slack to find other hackers looking for teammates, and pull up to our in-person team mixer event right after the Opening Ceremony in the **Lower Sproul Tent**\!  
- Make sure to add your teammates on Devpost during submission, there is nothing else required to formally declare a team. All teammates must be confirmed hackers.

## 

## Project Submission & Judging {#project-submission-&-judging}

All completed hacker projects must be submitted on Devpost by 11:00 AM, on Sunday morning. You may continue to hack and edit your submission until **12:00 PM**, after which we will not be accepting any more edits.  We HIGHLY recommend hackers create a draft Devpost by midnight on Saturday with all project teammates included and a project name, as this is the only way we can guarantee your project will be judged.

***Late projects will not be considered for judging, and you MUST be in-person at the venue for the judging period to be eligible for judging*****.** 

**All projects must be started during the event.** Sadly, we disqualify a number of entries every year for violating this rule. Your project should also follow our hacker [Code of Conduct](https://hackberkeley.org/conduct.pdf).

Judges will visit project teams between 1:00pm to 3:00pm; this will be your chance to show off the amazing projects you’ve built\! You must stay at your table the entire time\! If your entire team is not present while a judge comes, you will not be judged\!  We recommend preparing a four minute pitch and any answers to possible questions\! Projects will be judged on a variety of factors, including impact, quality, technical complexity, and creativity. Our judges know that one day is a short timeframe and that many of our hackers are beginners, so don’t feel pressured to create the next BeReal — rather, we love to see projects that creatively attempt to tackle a problem, and care more about the idea, *genuine effort*, and thought process behind the project rather than the final product. 

There will be 1 Grand Prize winning team for each track: Ddoski’s World, Ddoski’s Toolbox, Ddoski’s Lab, and Ddoski’s Playground (more on these below). Many of our sponsors will also be offering prizes for creative implementation of their products/APIs; check the Sponsor Prizes section for more information\!

During the event, we will be sending out a detailed document describing the judging process and what we’re looking for in projects. Stay tuned\!

## Judging: Final Round {#judging:-final-round}

If you are one of the 10 teams selected to pitch your project on stage, your team representative will receive a call on Sunday, 6/22 \~3:15pm. You will be asked to make your way to a specified room, where you will receive further instructions.

You should expect to present a 3 minute presentation during the closing ceremony on the Wheeler Auditorium stage, with a 2 minute Q\&A from our expert VC and research judging panel. 

## 

## Communication {#communication}

Slack will be your primary source for announcements, mentorship, team formation, and more. You will miss things if you are not on Slack\! If you haven’t already, please join Slack at the link below:

[Click Here to Join Slack\!](https://hackberkeley.org/slack)

- Got a question for mentors? Head to \#1-ask-mentors for help\! We will also have a ticketing system on Slack where you can fill out a detailed support request for mentors.  
- Here are some ground rules for Slack:  
  * Ask questions in \#0-ask-directors. Please refrain from @mentioning any specific director with questions in other channels\!   
  * If a situation is potentially life-threatening, call **911**.   
  * Treat others with kindness\! Toxic or hateful messages will not be tolerated, and anyone breaking the Code of Conduct / ground rules will be removed from the hackathon.  
  * Check pinned messages in \#0-faq\! Your question may have already been answered\!   
  * Use threads when you can to keep relevant topics in one message. 

## 

## Hacker Pass {#hacker-pass}

In addition to your lanyard/badge and wristband which you will get at check-in, your Hacker Pass will be your ticket throughout the weekend. You will need this to check-in, grab food and swag, and check in to various events during the hackathon. Your Hacker Pass is available for download on your [**Hive portal**](https://hive.hackberkeley.org/wallet-pass) and in the Hackathons @ Berkeley app (more on that below\!).

**Please download (add to Apple Wallet / Google Wallet) your Hacker Pass now so you have it ready for the event\!**

## 

## Hackathons @ Berkeley App {#hackathons-@-berkeley-app}

The Hackathons @ Berkeley App is the **official companion app** for AI Hackathon 2026\! Use it as a perfect hive to:

* View the full hackathon schedule  
* Search and filter for events by type and timing  
* Bookmark events for reminders  
* Explore venue maps and linked event locations  
* Receive important announcements and updates  
* Access your digital badge and wallet pass

Please download the app now at one of the following links (or by searching “Hackathons @ Berkeley” in the App Store / Play Store):

* App Store: [https://apps.apple.com/us/app/hackathons-berkeley/id6761351929](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/hackathons-berkeley/id6761351929)  
* Play Store: [https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=io.calhacks.hive](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=io.calhacks.hive)

## 

## Grand Prizes & Tracks {#grand-prizes-&-tracks}

The AI Hackathon 2026 has four grand prizes for its four tracks\! The winning teams for each track will receive **$5,000 in cash**.

**Ddoski’s World Track:** This track is for projects at the intersection of technology and social impact. This can include (but is not limited to) EdTech tools that improve access to quality education, civic platforms that support political participation or community organizing, environmental apps that track, reduce, or communicate ecological impact, and software that addresses systemic inequity in any form. If your project is motivated by a real-world social challenge, this is where it belongs.

**Ddoski’s Toolbox Track:** This track covers tools built for developers, creators, and knowledge workers. Think developer utilities, APIs, automation scripts, workflow apps, project management systems, writing or design aids, and anything else that meaningfully improves how people work or create. We are interested in how useful, usable, and well-executed your tools are — not just the idea behind them.

**Ddoski’s Lab Track:** This track is for projects rooted in science and engineering. This can include health tech and medical tools, hardware hacks, embedded systems, biotech applications, data-driven scientific research tools, and solutions to complex engineering problems. Projects can be software, hardware, or a combination — what unites them is a grounding in technical depth and real-world application.

**Ddoski’s Playground Track:** AI is changing the creative space. Think outside of the box because this is our most open ended track\! If you’re looking for an idea, have a think about how you could use AI to produce art or music or help others who do. Alternatively, have a go at building an experience like a game or performance. Some examples can include games, interactive experiences, generative art, experimental interfaces, and any idea that's creative or unconventional in its approach.

Each team will be able to apply to exactly **one** main track. In addition to the four main tracks, there are a few other prizes that Hackathons @ Berkeley will be offering. All teams will automatically be considered for these prizes. The prizes that we are offering are summarized below:

## Hackathon @ Berkeley Prizes 

| Track Name | Track Criteria | Prize(s) |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Grand Prize: Ddoski’s World | Best project in the track, selected after finalist presentations. | $5K cash prize |
| Grand Prize: Ddoski’s Toolbox | Best project in the track, selected after finalist presentations. | $5K cash prize |
| Grand Prize: Ddoski’s Lab | Best project in the track, selected after finalist presentations. | $5K cash prize |
| Grand Prize: Ddoski’s Playground | Best project in the track, selected after finalist presentations. | $5K cash prize |
| Finalists (top 10 teams) | For all finalists invited to present on-stage at the Closing Ceremony. | Limited edition AI Hackathon 2026 merch |
| SkyDeck Grand Prize Winner | One startup-oriented Grand Prize winner will be invited to join SkyDeck's incubator program\! | Guaranteed admission to SkyDeck’s Pad-13 incubator program |
| Best UI/UX | Unique and Innovative  UI/UX designs | Kodak Chemera Cameras |
| Best Solo Hack | Highly Received Solo Hack | XGIMI Projector |
| Most Technical Hack | Most technically advanced. | Flashforge 3D Printers |
| Best Golden Bear Hack | For the best project that addresses a challenge faced by Berkeley students. | $100 Cal Student Store gift cards |
| Best Beginner Hack | To be eligible, all members of your team must have attended 0 prior hackathons. | AirPods 4 Noise Cancellation  |
| Hacker’s Choice | Voted on by hackers during judging\! | Mechanical Keyboards |
| Most Questionable Use of 24 Hours | Anything but hacking… | Lego Sets |

## 

## Sponsor Tracks {#sponsor-tracks}

Many of our sponsors are offering prize tracks for the best & most creative uses of their tools. These are summarized below. More information, including the Hacker Starter Pack for each track that you will need to get started, will be posted on our Live Site at the start of the hackathon. Note that these prizes are subject to change.

**We *highly* encourage you to participate in at least one sponsor track\!** Our sponsors bring immense value and state-of-the-art technologies to your hackathon experience that will be at your fingertips this weekend. Furthermore, sponsors very frequently recruit straight from students they meet at hackathons\!

| Sponsor | Track Criteria | Prize(s) |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Annapurna Labs (Co-Host)** | Coming soon\! | Coming soon\! |
| **Fetch AI (Co-Host)** | Coming soon\! | Cash ($1500, $1000, $500) |
| Midjourney | Coming soon\! | Coming soon\! |
| Interaction Co | Technical depth of the integration, usefulness of the automation, and overall  hack originality. | Coming soon\! |
| The Token Company | Depth of research, ingenuity, creativity. | Claude Code, Money, Merch |
| Redis | \- Using Redis Beyond Caching: leveraging our AI tools like Redis Iris for Agent memory, vector search, context retrieval \- Creativity and originality: solving real human problems in ways we haven't seen before, has a fun factor \- Technical Implementation: Quality and sophistication of the engineering, Correctness, scalability, and architecture. | Mac Minis, 25k Redis Cloud credits for the team, Redis Timbuk2 backpacks |
| Anthropic | Our tech prize this year celebrates teams that reach the furthest: projects built with Claude Code that tackle meaningful issues in health, education, economic opportunity, or any domain where AI could genuinely shift what's possible for people. We know you have limited time, but aspiration and effort matter more than the outcome this time, so take the biggest swing toward the most challenging problem you can think of\! | $5000 in API credits (total), an office hour with our Applied AI team, and an invitation to visit our office in SF. |
| Browserbase | Participants are tasked with building any agent that uses the web \- any framework, harness, model \- but it must be powered by the Browserbase platform. Must use one of the following: browsers, search, fetch, Stagehand, Browse CLI. | Coming soon\! |
| Orkes | 1\. How did they use Agentspan to build an agent or bring an agent 2\. How well did they integrate Agentspan into their demos | Ray Ban Metas |
| Simular | Projects must use a Simular product (Sai, SimuLang, or Agent S) in a meaningful way and post about the experience on X or LinkedIn, tagging @official accounts. Judges will pick the winning team based on creativity (bonus points for novel use cases), technical execution, and real-world impact. (Optional if the organizer has other submission channel \- to be considered, email zening@simular.ai with your team name, project description, social post link, and a short note on how you used Simular product). | Prize package worth $500 |
| PaleBlueDot AI | Coming soon\! | Coming soon\! |
| QNX | Hard requirements: \- Product uses QNX OS \-Product includes one of the open-source AI modules available at https://oss.qnx.com Judging criteria: \- Is it a ”cannot-fail” embedded application? \- Does it require real-time or reliability? \- Is it using AI in an interesting or creative way? \- Is it running on the embedded hardware (and not in cloud)? | $1000 main winner, 2 x $250 runner-up prizes |
| Arize | Evidence that Arize was used and actually improved the application. | $1k cash (delivered as gift cards) |
| Overshoot AI | Coming soon\! | Coming soon\! |
| Fieldguide | Coming soon\! | Coming soon\! |
| Deepgram | The Deepgram prize goes to the team that builds the most creative and well-executed voice-powered experience using Deepgram's APIs. We'll judge on creativity of the voice interaction, technical execution, and how essential voice is to the experience (rather than tacked on). Submitted projects must demonstrably use at least one Deepgram product (speech-to-text, text-to-speech, or the voice agent API). | Nintendo Switch 2 for each member of the team |
| Sentry | Sentry is looking for builders who go beyond the prompt, we want to see strong technical execution paired with clear communication, collaborative problem-solving, and the confidence to speak up, course-correct, and lead when it counts. The best submissions will demonstrate not just what you built, but how your team worked together under pressure to make it happen. Bonus points if you leveraged observability or error monitoring in your project(s), we love engineers who think about reliability from day one. | Nintendo Switch 2 System for each member of the team |
| Pika | \- Creativity and originality \- Effective use of Pika MCP and AI workflows \- Quality and impact of the final output | First: 500 \+ office visit \+ Pika MCP credit 10k \+ Merch; Second: 300 \+ office visit \+ Pika MCP credit 10k Third: 200+ office visit \+ Pika MCP credit 5k |
| Cognition | Most interesting & technically impressive project built with Devin. | Devin credits \+ TBD |
| Band | We would like to provide the prize to the top project overall that used Band as a key technology in their project. So we rely on the regular judging for ranking projects, just pick the top one that used Band in a significant way (at least 2 agents collaborating via the BAND platform). | $1000 overall in amazon gift cards, split equally to winners. Prize to be distributed post hackathon. |
| Ultimate Bots | $3,000 Best Physical AI Hack — one prize, one winner. Open to any team, stack, or direction, as long as it's physical AI: code that closes the loop with a real or simulated robot, or generates, curates, or evaluates the data and worlds that train one. Submissions via Devpost on Sunday (≤3-min demo video, public repo or doc, 200-word writeup); the judges' test: would a real robotics team use it? | $3,000 cash prize, plus top 4 Ghost Trials performers invited to perform on stage at our June 26 SF show. |
| Cognichip | Projects will be evaluated on their creative and technically sound use of the Cognichip Artificial Chip Intelligence™ platform to solve a real chip design problem. Judges will assess design methodology and execution, quality of the final deliverable, and how effectively the team leveraged AI to accelerate the design process. | Tiny tapeout credits |
| Terac | Build a simple annotation app, use Terac to collect real human-labeled data from our panel, then fine-tune a model on that data and show it beats the base model. To be eligible, your training data must come from annotations collected through Terac during the event, not synthetic or pre-existing datasets. Projects are judged on improvement over the base model shown to hold on unseen examples (50%), the creativity and UX of your annotation environment (30%), and how smartly you used the human data within your $250 credit (20%). | 1000$ (1st place), 400$ (2nd place) |

# 

# Workshops & Entertainment {#workshops-&-entertainment}

## Workshops {#workshops}

Many of our sponsors will be running workshops throughout Saturday. They will be introducing how to create a project using their tools, explaining their prize track, and many of them will be providing credits and swag. We highly recommend you to attend as many of them as possible\!

The following sponsors will be running workshops. The detailed schedule will be viewable on the **live site** and **app** once it’s released\!

| Sponsor | Workshop Description |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Annapurna Labs (Co-Host)** | Coming soon\! |
| **Fetch AI (Co-Host)** | This hands-on workshop focuses on turning ideas into real, discoverable AI agents. We’ll walk you through practical use cases and showcase project ideas that align with the hackathon challenge. From designing agentic workflows to registering agents on Agentverse and making them discoverable via ASI:One, you’ll see the full lifecycle in action. Beyond building, we’ll dive into monetisation, integrating Chat Protocol and Payment Protocol, exposing your agents to real users, and transforming capabilities into revenue-generating services.By the end of the session, you’ll have a clear blueprint for creating, deploying, and monetising AI agents that solve real-world problems and a strong foundation to build a winning hackathon project. |
| Midjourney | Coming soon\! |
| Interaction Co | Technical workshop on building 3p miniapps on poke. Learn to build, deploy, and integrate your own agentic tools. |
| The Token Company | Coming soon\! |
| Redis | In this hands-on workshop you'll learn how to supercharge your LLM app with three powerful Redis systems that show up in nearly every production AI app. Starting from a simple chatbot, you'll build up a full pipeline that uses vector search to ground responses in real data (RAG), semantic caching to skip redundant LLM API calls and cut latency from seconds to milliseconds, and Agent Memory to give your chatbot a persistent, searchable memory across conversation turns. By the end, you'll have a working AI assistant — "Hack Buddy" — that you can fork and remix for your own hackathon project, plus a solid mental model for when and why to reach for each Redis feature in your own AI stack. |
| Anthropic | Coming soon\! |
| Context | Coming soon\! |
| Browserbase | Learn how to use the Browserbase CLI. |
| Orkes | Coming soon\! |
| Simular | Human vibe coding is the past \- let agents vibe code for you. In this hands-on workshop, you'll learn vibe hacking — completing an entire hackathon project from scratch without writing a single line of code. Meet Sai, Simular's AI coworker that uses your computer the way you do (clicking, typing, browsing, deploying) and orchestrates Claude Code to vibe-code on your behalf. Unlike other AI coding tools, Sai then tests its own work — clicking through the app it just built, catching bugs, and iterating the build → test → fix loop until your project actually works. Bring an idea, leave with a shipped app. No CS background required. |
| PaleBlueDot AI | Coming soon\! |
| QNX | Coming soon\! |
| Arize | Arize AI is a way to log what an AI agent is up to, test it, and automatically improve it by taking test outputs and feeding them back to your coding agent. You can vibe code a better app faster with Arize AI. |
| Overshoot AI | Coming soon\! |
| Fieldguide | Coming soon\! |
| Deepgram | Build a working voice agent in 40 minutes. We'll start from a Deepgram starter app and modify it three ways so you can drop voice into your hackathon project today. |
| Sentry | Coming soon\! |
| Pika | Discover how to turn Claude into a creative partner using the Pika MCP. See real examples, learn practical workflows, and get hands-on experience creating videos, ads, and other content for your own projects. |
| Cognition | Learn how the most AI-pilled engineers build software today. We'll give a demo of Devin, show how we personally use it, and walk through our favorite AI engineering tips. |
| Band | In this hands-on workshop, students will learn how to build multi-agent AI apps with Band: create agents that coordinate in shared rooms, exchange context, and collaborate on tasks they can extend into their hackathon projects. |
| Ultimate Bots | Train real robot policies on the Nebius Physical AI Workbench and compete for $3,000 — or just record your moves in Ultimate Bots Studio and watch a real humanoid perform them outside. Free $150 compute per team. |

## 

## Pre-Event Workshop Recording

Fetch AI ran a pre-event workshop on the Fetch AI suite\! The workshop provided an overview of the Fetch.ai ecosystem, its core components, and its capabilities. Participants gained insights into the hackathon's challenge statement and received guidance on how to approach and solve it using Fetch.ai technology.

Please see the recording of the workshop here: [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=TRZ6cfvUPC4](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=TRZ6cfvUPC4)

## **Entertainment** {#entertainment}

**The following entertainment events will be taking place:**

- Inflatable Obstacle Course  
- Llamas  
- Big C Sunset Hike   
- Clash Royale Tournament  
- Silent Disco  
- Cheese Tasting  
- Matcha Popup  
- Poker Night

 … and more\! Check out the live site for the times and more details\!

## 

## 

## Hardware \+ Hardware Rental Booth {#hardware-+-hardware-rental-booth}

We are excited to announce several opportunities to work on hardware projects at AI Hackathon\! See the opportunities below.

We will be running a Hardware Rental Booth\! It will consist of:

- Arduino Uno R3s  
- Various common Arduino [accessories](https://www.amazon.com/dp/B07CTFVM1L?ref=fed_asin_title) & [modules](https://www.amazon.com/HiLetgo-Sensors-Assortment-Raspberry-Projects/dp/B01N5910XS/ref=sr_1_4?crid=2E0EELR6IPZDJ&dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.vGc9dihS_dS94eu7Qg8-MopDNgsMSkv16F31QUIpr2FCkr3lM2coVwc7sRrWdsiEwlgGbI4WnKPmiWiO7p9uLYUFjOkWONco308RCSyrmyi_I6-Ajb_JOYzd8thS3rE2_P0oiIEoMmKLvDTguMw0oydfnYXG1tMguVQ27GGMGwZaPWuNKETMYMpky_-oE84u8vcM3dGpKFxTpE_-Ymt_yaycwkOJK9Rz15s804YNm8Y.hGtzY418ki3nIhSxlhTgWepLjIgjmULPOHPyiE8FWUc&dib_tag=se&keywords=arduino+sensor+kit&qid=1760700945&sprefix=arduino+sensor+kit%2Caps%2C152&sr=8-4)  
- Arducam & LogiTech camera modules  
- MPU-6050s  
- ESP32s  
- Raspberry Pi 4s \- 4GB (w/ MicroSD cards & MicroHDMI to HDMI adapters)  
- Raspberry Pi Picos  
- Soldering irons, solder, & various soldering accessories

Hackers will be able to check out items using their Hacker Pass starting at 11:15 AM on Saturday. Items are in a **limited supply** and will be **first come, first served**. Items must be returned by 4pm on Sunday (the start of the closing ceremony), unless your team is selected as a finalist, in which case you will be permitted to keep your borrowed parts. If you already have your own hardware parts, we encourage you to bring them to guarantee that you have all the parts you’ll need.

**Note** that if you plan on renting a Raspberry Pi, you will need to supply your own keyboard, mouse, and monitor, should you need those items.

**3D Printing Station**

We will have three 3D printers and PLA filament in an assortment of colors\! There will be a form to get in line to use these.

**Soldering Station**

We will have a soldering station on Lower Sproul Plaza with solder and 3-4 soldering irons. Please do not remove anything from the soldering station. Note that soldering cannot occur inside the venue due to safety regulations.

**Hardware Opportunities from Sponsors\!**

Several sponsors will be offering hardware opportunities in their prize tracks. Namely:

* Ultimate Bots: humanoid robotics track\!  
  * Please fill out the [hardware interest form](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScVTDZNxp2JyE6rBGe4r3jxPrFlMIfq40UAGrm3iNX9OZJ2rg/viewform) so we can contact you directly about this opportunity.  
* Cognichip: chip design track\!  
  * Please fill out [this interest form](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScXr6FGaOc-LUajk9nnFXSYjjfaE4WEas9BtvoOMY3OrXIgWA/viewform) to receive more information from Cognichip.

Please attend their workshops, visit their booth, and message in their Sponsor Slack channels to learn more\!

# Emergency Information {#emergency-information}

If there is an urgent emergency/immediate threat to life, call 911. 

The nearest hospital is Alta Bates Summit Medical Hospital located at **2001 Dwight Way, Berkeley, CA 94704.**

We will not be providing any form of medication other than basic first aid in cases of emergencies. Please bring your own medication if you require any. 

For immediate hackathon-related emergencies, contact a director through the \#0-ask-directors channel on slack. If it’s a serious emergency or involves private information, go to the director help desk staffed 24/7 located on the 2nd floor, across from Goldie’s.

For non-urgent situations or questions, seek out a Hackathons @ Berkeley director (wearing a black shirt) or ask in the \#0-ask-directors channel on Slack.  We’ll point you in the right direction\! 

# 

# 

# 

# FAQs {#faqs}

* Do I need to show up for all 2 days?  
  While the hackathon experience is incomplete without attending the entirety of the event, you can arrive late or leave early if you have other commitments. However, you will need to be in-person for the judging portion of the hackathon\!  
    
* I have a dietary restriction. Will you be able to accommodate?   
  We will be offering vegetarian / vegan / gluten free options as a part of our meals\! Due to the vast variety of dietary restrictions, however, we are unable to completely account for specific allergies or restrictions. For non-vegetarian hackers, we ask that you avoid grabbing vegetarian meals if possible to ensure those with dietary restrictions are able to eat\!  
    
* Are hackers required to stay overnight?  
  Nope\! Many of our hackers are staying in different nearby hotels or other accommodations. Please use the \#1-travel-buddies channel if you would like to coordinate potential accommodations with other hackers. Though staying overnight is a hackathon tradition, do whatever you feel most comfortable with\!  
    
* Will Ddoski (aka. huge teddy bear \+ our beloved mascot) be there?   
  It wouldn’t be a Hackathons @ Berkeley event without him.  
    
* I lost something or found something someone lost, is there a lost and found area to retrieve or deposit it?  
  Yes\! Come down to the director help desk where directors will be stationed to help you retrieve anything that has been deposited at the desk.   
    
* Is there wifi at the event?  
  Absolutely, the wifi network name is Berkeley-Visitor and it does not require a password. Eduroam is also available if you already have it from another school, we will not be able to grant access otherwise.  
    
* Is there a preferred tool or programming language to build my project in?  
  Nope\! Build whatever you want\! 


  
Made with 💙 by the Hackathons at Berkeley team\!
